resources.string: Kotlin is the official language for Android development. Focus on the difference between val and var, and master Null Safety.
Understand when onPause or onStop are called. This helps avoid memory leaks and bugs during configuration changes.
Learn how to build complex interfaces without nesting. Chains and Guidelines are essential for performance.
Learn to display large datasets. Using ViewHolder prevents unnecessary findViewById calls.
Don\'t guess why the app crashed. Learn to read StackTraces and filter logs by application tags.
Vector images (SVG/XML) don\'t lose quality when scaled and weigh much less than raster images (PNG/JPG).
View Binding creates type-safe references to layout elements, eliminating NullPointerExceptions when accessing Views.
Use MaterialCardView, buttons, and text fields according to MD3 standards for a native and beautiful UI.
ViewModel allows you to store screen state data that doesn\'t disappear when the device rotates or the theme changes.
Use observable objects to automatically update the interface when data changes in the database or network.
Perform heavy tasks (network, DB) off the main thread to keep the application interface smooth.
Retrofit is the de facto standard for HTTP requests. It automatically turns JSON responses into Kotlin objects.
Room is a wrapper over SQLite that allows you to conveniently save app data directly on the device.
Use NavGraph to visualize paths between fragments and activities. This simplifies argument passing.
DI helps make code testable and modular. Hilt is the easiest way to implement DI in an Android project.
DataStore is a modern storage for simple settings (key-value), working asynchronously via Flow.
Use WorkManager for tasks that must be executed even if the app is closed (e.g., data synchronization).
Always check permissions at runtime (camera, location) and explain to the user why the app needs them.
Write tests for ViewModels and repositories to ensure the correctness of your calculations.
Use adaptive colors in resources (values-night) so the app looks great in any lighting.
Use R8 to remove unused code and obfuscate it to protect the app and reduce APK size.
Publish apps to Google Play in Bundle format — this allows users to download only the resources needed for their screen.
Add smooth transitions between screens using MotionLayout or simple property animations (ObjectAnimator).
Memory leaks lead to lags and crashes. LeakCanary helps quickly find forgotten references to Context or Views.
Move all strings to strings.xml. This allows you to easily translate the app into other languages in the future.
Don\'t load images manually. These libraries handle caching and optimize memory consumption.
Separate code into layers (Data, Domain, UI). This makes the project scalable and easy to maintain.
Use fragments to create adaptive designs for tablets and smartphones within a single Activity.
Use the built-in Android Studio tool to analyze View hierarchy in real-time.
Prepare screenshots, description, and an icon. Check the app for compliance with Google Play policies before uploading.
